name: Khavda Renewable Energy Park
blurb: A renewable park the size of a city rising from the Rann of Kutch salt desert — ~30 GW of solar and wind planned, several GW already feeding the grid.

name: Dogger Bank Wind Farm
blurb: Three phases, 3.6 GW, ~130 km off the Yorkshire coast — set to be the world's largest offshore wind farm, with turbines taller than the Eiffel Tower's second level.

name: Jiuquan Wind Power Base
blurb: A wind megabase strung across the Gobi Desert wind corridor — thousands of turbines, more capacity than many countries' entire grids.

name: Bhadla Solar Park
blurb: 2.2 GW of panels across Rajasthan's Thar desert; from above it reads as a blue lake in the sand. Among the cheapest electricity ever contracted anywhere.

name: Noor Ouarzazate Solar Complex
blurb: Concentrated solar towers and mirror fields at the gate of the Sahara, storing heat in molten salt to keep generating after sunset.

name: Lake Turkana Wind Power
blurb: 365 turbines in Kenya's Rift Valley, threaded through a natural wind funnel between two mountain ranges — around 15% of the country's electricity.

name: SunZia Wind
blurb: 3.5 GW across New Mexico plus a 550-mile HVDC line to carry it west — the largest clean-energy infrastructure project in US history.

name: Hollandse Kust Zuid
blurb: 1.5 GW in the Dutch North Sea, built entirely without subsidy — a milestone moment for offshore wind economics.

name: Moss Landing Energy Storage
blurb: A retired gas plant site on Monterey Bay turned into one of the world's largest battery installations, storing solar for California evenings.

name: Dalian Flow Battery
blurb: The world's largest vanadium flow battery — energy stored in tanks of liquid electrolyte that can cycle daily for decades without wearing out.
